conversation_log.summary: Task is well-structured and thorough, but proposes Docker file locations that contradict the canonical folder structure defined in architecture.md and ADR-004. The architecture specifies: docker-compose.yml at docker/docker-compose.yml (not repo root), Dockerfiles at docker/Dockerfile.frontend and docker/Dockerfile.backend (not apps/*/Dockerfile). ADR-004 Rule #4 states: 'docker/ contains all Docker-related files. No Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml at the repo root.' The task must be updated to place all Docker files in the docker/ directory and adjust volume mount paths, build contexts, and README instructions accordingly. The docker compose up command is run from docker/, not the repo root. All other aspects (service names, networking, env vars, dev-only builds, named volumes) are architecturally sound.

5 acceptance criteria require revision before implementation. (1) AC #1: wrong file path — references repo root instead of docker/docker-compose.yml. (2) AC #3: wrong working directory — references running from repo root instead of docker/. (3) AC #6: vague and unobservable — no specific verification mechanism defined. (4) AC #7: vague and unobservable — no concrete request, URL, or expected response defined. (5) AC #8: wrong Dockerfile paths. AC #9 and AC #10 also mechanically inaccurate (volume mounts declared in compose file, not Dockerfiles).

Revised all acceptance criteria to align with docker/ directory convention. Corrected file paths, working directories, Dockerfile locations, added DATABASE_URL to .env.example keys, replaced vague connectivity criteria with observable binary outcomes, corrected volume-mount attribution to compose file.

Revised criteria align with architecture.md and ADR-004. One remaining correction: database service name must be 'postgres' not 'db' in AC #2 and AC #6, per architecture.md Directory Responsibilities.
3 criteria remain untestable: AC #2, #5, #6 still reference service name 'db' instead of 'postgres'. All other previously-flagged criteria resolved.
Applied db->postgres naming corrections in AC #2, #5, and #6. All 15 acceptance criteria now fully revised.

Final unconditional approval. All 15 revised acceptance criteria fully aligned with canonical architecture. Implementation constraints: postgres image version 16+, DATABASE_URL uses 'postgres' hostname, all Docker files exclusively in docker/.
All 15 acceptance criteria are testable as written. db->postgres corrections in AC #2, #5, #6 confirmed applied. Full test method table provided for all criteria.

Initial implementation on branch task/10-docker-compose-local-dev. Authored docker/docker-compose.yml (postgres service name, bind-mounts, named volume, user-defined network, healthchecks), .env.example (all 6 required keys with postgres hostname in DATABASE_URL), and README.md (Local Development section). PR #20 opened.

Added three missing files to PR #20: (1) docker/Dockerfile.frontend — dev-only Node.js 20 Alpine; installs monorepo workspace deps at build time; runs npm run dev --host 0.0.0.0; source bind-mounted at runtime; no production stage. (2) docker/Dockerfile.backend — dev-only Node.js 20 Alpine; installs monorepo workspace deps at build time; runs npm run dev; source bind-mounted at runtime; no production stage. (3) .gitignore — ignores .env, node_modules/, dist/, .turbo/, TS build info, editor artefacts. All 15 acceptance criteria now satisfied. agent:tester label applied to PR #20.

decisions.approach: docker/docker-compose.yml, .env.example, and README.md were authored correctly on the branch (postgres service naming, bind-mounts, named volume, user-defined network, healthchecks). docker/Dockerfile.frontend, docker/Dockerfile.backend, and .gitignore were missing and added in the second developer pass.
risks: apps/frontend and apps/backend directories do not yet exist (Task #1 dependency). Bind-mount volumes in docker-compose.yml will fail until those directories are created by the monorepo scaffold task.

Both Dockerfiles COPY package manifests for packages/tsconfig and packages/contracts at build time — if those workspace packages do not yet exist, the COPY instructions will fail at image build time. Ordering dependency on the monorepo scaffold task.

node_modules anonymous volumes prevent the host bind-mount from shadowing container-installed packages. Developers must run 'docker compose build' after any package.json dependency changes rather than a host-side npm install.
